vulnerability.title: Verify Permissions on /var/log/messages File
vulnerability.description: To properly set the permissions of /var/log/messages, run the command: $ sudo chmod 0600 /var/log/messages

triage_decision.reason: File permissions changes are explicitly marked as safe_to_remediate in policy, as they do not typically cause lockout or system breakage. This change restricts access to /var/log/messages to the owner only, enhancing security without affecting critical services like SSH or authentication.

llm_verdict.message:
## Remediation Summary

**Finding:** `file_permissions_var_log_messages` (Severity: 2)  
**File:** `/var/log/messages`  
**Action:** Set permissions to `0600` (owner read/write only)

### Before:
```
-rwxrwxrwx. 1 root root 5491956 Apr  7 23:11 /var/log/messages
```

### After:
```
-rw-------. 1 root root 5504058 Apr  7 23:17 /var/log/messages
```

The permissions have been successfully restricted to the owner only, addressing the security finding. The change is minimal, reversible, and does not affect critical services like SSH. The verification scan will run automatically to confirm the fix.

$FileCreateMode 0600
